title: Twitter @kyrylo
summary: When N+1 queries in SQLite actually save the day: https://t.co/VM3aLijK2Y

title: Twitter @fractaledmind
summary: Yes! SQLite is *completely* different than PG or MySQL. With a client/server db, you want a small number of large queries to reduce network overhead. With an embedded db, you want a large number of small queries to maximize indexes and in-memory data.

title: ZeQLplus
summary:
Open Source Terminal SQLite Database Browser 
 Pre-built binaries for macOS, Linux, Windows 10 are available as zip files in the releaseshttpsgithub.comZetloStudioZeQLplusreleases page. Just extract and run directly with no need to install. From the command line in a Terminal CMD window shell zeql Note you should move the zeql executable to a location in your path.

title: sql.js-httpvfs
summary:
Hosting read-only SQLite databases on static file hosters like Github Pages 
 See my blog post for an introduction httpsphiresky.github.ioblog2021hosting-sqlite-databases-on-github-pages sql.js is a light wrapper around SQLite compiled with EMScripten for use in the browser client-side. This repo is a fork of and wrapper around sql.js to provide a read-only HTTP-Range-request based virtual file system for SQLite. It allows hosting an SQLite database on a static file hoster and querying that data

title: turso
summary:
Turso Database is a project to build the next evolution of SQLite. 
 Turso Database Turso Database is an in-process SQL database, compatible with SQLite. Turso Database is a work-in-progress, in-process OLTP database engine library written in Rust that has The following features are on our current roadmap Please see the Turso Database Manualdocsmanual.md for more information.
